title: cyanea macrostegia subsp. gibsonii
intro: Cyanea macrostegia subsp. gibsonii, also known as the 'Hilo hibiscus' or 'Gibson's cyanea', is a rare and endangered Hawaiian plant species belonging to the hibiscus family. The plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cyanea macrostegia subsp. gibsonii include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as supporting immune function and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing its leaves in hot water or using them in tinctures, and are often used in traditional Hawaiian medicine to promote overall well-being.
